problem:
It can be shown that for any positive integer $n,$
\[\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}^n = \begin{pmatrix} F_{n + 1} & F_n \\ F_n & F_{n - 1} \end{pmatrix},\]where $F_n$ denotes the $n$th Fibonacci number.

Compute $F_{784} F_{786} - F_{785}^2.$

classified_questions.question: What is the given formula that relates the power of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}$ to the Fibonacci numbers?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we use the formula to find the determinant of $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}^{785}$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How does the determinant of $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}^{785}$ relate to $F_{784} F_{786} - F_{785}^2$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify the determinant of $\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}^{785}$ using the given formula?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the value of $F_{784} F_{786} - F_{785}^2$ that we obtain from applying the formula and simplifying the determinant?
classified_questions.label: b
